item.title: research
item.description: Research interests Transit spectroscopy and exoplanetary atmospheres Magnetism of cool stars High-resolution near-infrared spectroscopy High-resolution optical spectropolarimetry and Zeeman-Doppler imaging Instrumentation Refereed publications Lavail, A.; Kochukhov, O.; Hussain, G.; Argiroffi, C.; Alecian, E.; Morin, J.; the BinaMIcS collaboration: The large-scale magnetic field of the eccentric pre-main-sequence binary system V1878 Ori. 2020, MNRAS, 497, 632. ADS | DOI Lavail, A.; Kochukhov, O.; Hussain, G.: Characterising the surface magnetic fields of T Tauri stars with high-resolution near-infrared spectroscopy.

item.title: AD Leo. Observations from 2019
item.description: LSD Profiles We got 6 observations in 2019. Stokes V profiles shown below. It looks like amplitude-wise, the profiles are recovered most of the pre-2016 amplitude. The phase coverage is not great, we have four observations clustered at phase 0.7-0.8, one at 0.0, one around 0.5: 0.00 0.46 2.72 1.80 1.81 1.81 ZDI I ran ZDI on the data anyhow, following same approach than for 2012 and 2016 data, with the same Stokes I filling factor value, regularisation parameters values, and Stokes V filling factor values.

item.title: about
item.description: Interests A bunch of unsorted keywords reflecting some of the things I do or take interest in. feminism, environment, hiking, vegetables, democracy, beer-brewing, analog photography, anticipation books, world literature, cycling, trip-hop, post-rock, nature, mountains, cross-country skiing, stars, listener-supported radios, baking pizzas, magnetic fields, dystopias, space, astronomy, sharing is caring, design, typography. Design Some of my duties at Uppsala University have been to design websites and posters for conferences and outreach events.

item.title: cv
item.description: Here you can download my academic CV (PDF document) The list of my publications can be found at this link (redirects to the ADS).

item.title: appearances
item.description: 2020 2020-05-26 - Public PhD defense at Polhemsalen, Ångströmlaboratoriet, Uppsala University (Sweden) 2019 2019.10.24 - Astronomdagarna 2019 at Stockholm University (Sweden) - poster 2019.08.05 - Preliminary acceptance Europe testing of the CRIRES+ polarimeter at ESO Garching (Germany) 2019.06.17 - STARRY final conference at Leeds (United Kingdom) - talk 2019.04.01 - 1-week science visit and seminar at Université de Montpellier (France) 2018 2018.07.29 - Cool Stars 20 at Boston / Cambridge (USA) - poster 2018.

item.title: A new logo for Svenska astronomiska sällskapet
A sudden change of the global magnetic field of the active M-dwarf AD Leo
item.description: Alexis Lavail, Oleg Kochukhov, and Gregg Wade A poster presented at the Cool Stars 20 conference Useful links PDF version of the poster Link to the paper on ADS Summary of the science in not-so-rigorous language Our aim was to get the first observations ever of a M-dwarf in all four Stokes parameters. This means one gets the intensity spectrum (Stokes I), the circularly polarized spectrum (Stokes V) and the two linearly polarized spectra (Stokes Q and U).

item.title: contact
item.description: If you’d like to get in touch / have any question, feel free to contact me! E-mail LinkedIn Visiting adress: room Å63413, Ångströmlaboratoriet, Lägerhyddsvägen 1, Uppsala

item.title: Presentations from the Uppsala-Stockholm PhD days
item.description: 10-11 april 2014, Uppsala Universitet Thursday 10 april 2014 Kai Yan Lee - Reionization and the cosmic dawn Hannes Jensen - Simulating observational probes of reionization Saghar Asadi - Gravitational millilensing as a probe of dark matter on subgalactic scales Beatriz Villarroel Rodriguez - What is an Active Galactic Nuclei? Sarah Jabbari - Origin of solar surface activity and sunspots Alexis Lavail - Investigation of stellar magnetic fields using infrared spectropolarimetry Lisa Rosén - Magnetic fields of cool active stars Naum Rusomarov - Magnetic fields of Ap stars from all four Stokes parameters Friday 11 april 2014 Andreas Sandberg - Observations of Lyman alpha emitting galaxies at low and intermediate redshifts Florent Duval - Lyman alpha radiative transfer in Star-burst Galaxies Johannes Puschnig - Multi-wavelength studies of star-forming galaxies Thøger Juul Rivera-Thorsen - Neutral gas kinematics and geometry in young, starforming galaxies and its impact on Lyman alpha radiation transport Samuel Regandell - Spectral Synthesis for protoplanetary disk models Gianni Cataldi - Observations and modeling of debris disks Sara Lindgren - Accurate spectroscopy of M dwarfs, a possible link to understand planet formation Erik Aronson - Spectroscopic characterization of exoplanetary atmospheres
